summary: corpus-hermeticum-15/-16 acquired as duplicate libellus XVI (Definitions of Asclepius); manifest labels disagree with chunking-config labels — sacred-texts 'th2' page->libellus mapping is irregular and needs re-mapping

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content:
Evidence (parent 50438e23, split B):
- Bodies: corpus-hermeticum-15 and -16 share 5/6 byte-identical chunk bodies; both are 'CORPUS HERMETICUM (XVI.) THE DEFINITIONS OF ASCLEPIUS UNTO KING AMMON'.
- Label disagreement: sources/manifest.toml says id=corpus-hermeticum-15 label='Corpus Hermeticum XV: The Definitions of Asclepius unto King Ammon' (url th231.htm) and id=corpus-hermeticum-16 (url th233.htm); but chunking/.../corpus-hermeticum-15.toml metadata says 'CH 15: The Hymn of Hermes' and -16 says 'CH 16: The Secret Sermon on the Mountain'. Manifest also has corpus-hermeticum-13 = 'XIII: The Secret Sermon on the Mountain'.
- Cause: sacred-texts.com 'th2' (Mead, Thrice-Greatest Hermes Vol.2) pages are NOT 1:1 with libelli; th231/th233 both resolve to / over-capture the Definitions of Asclepius. So the true CH-15 (Hymn of Hermes) and the chunking-config-labelled texts are missing, and metadata is internally inconsistent (Mead's own numbering vs the chunk-config numbering).
TODO for this ticket: (1) map each th2.htm page to its actual libellus from the Mead TOC; (2) fix manifest urls/labels + chunking metadata to agree; (3) re-acquire so each CH source-id holds its correct, distinct libellus; (4) re-chunk + verify no duplicate bodies across CH source-ids. Independent of the chunk.py apparatus fix in the parent.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: Elevated by the dossier plan (docs/summary/work-grouping.md): corpus-hermeticum-15/-16 duplicate changes the corpus-hermeticum WORK's member list (17 members as tabled). This is id-changing, so it must resolve BEFORE the span plan freezes for that work — otherwise the CH dossier bakes a duplicate libellus XVI into structure_json and the L2 summary. Blocks CH dossier generation only; rest of campaign 1 unaffected.
